title: aster novi-belgii subsp. tardiflorus
intro: Aster novi-belgii subsp. tardiflorus, also known as the late-flowering New York aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Aster novi-belgii subsp. tardiflorus include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or tinctures made from its flowers, leaves, or roots, which can be consumed as teas or used topically for skin conditions.
